flashcards.back: A memory management scheme where pages are loaded into physical memory only when they are accessed (on demand), rather than all at once at program start.
A type of interrupt (trap) raised by the hardware when a process tries to access a page that is mapped in its virtual address space but is not currently resident in physical memory.

flashcards.front: What is the role of the Valid-Invalid bit?
flashcards.back: A bit in each Page Table Entry (PTE) that tells the MMU whether the page is currently in RAM (Valid) or not in RAM/illegal (Invalid). An access to an Invalid page causes a page fault.
A severe performance degradation that occurs when the system spends more time swapping pages in and out of memory (servicing page faults) than it does executing useful instructions.

An optimization technique where processes share pages until one of them attempts to modify the page. At that point, a private copy of the page is created for the modifying process to maintain isolation.
